skill.name: laravel-security-audit
skill.description: Security auditor for Laravel applications. Analyzes code for vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, and insecure practices using OWASP standards and Laravel security best practices.
skill.summary: Review Laravel applications for authentication, authorization, validation, upload, API, XSS, database, and deployment security issues.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported code execution, network, credential, cryptographic, reconnaissance, and heuristic concerns. Manual review found only instructional audit checklist text and a PHP example, with no executable code, no outbound network behavior, and no credential access. The skill is safe to publish with low residual risk because it is a community security-review prompt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Detected as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 220 starts a fenced PHP example block, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. The surrounding lines show a static Laravel query example and no instruction to execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched line is a Markdown code fence for a PHP example. No shell command, Ruby expression, or execution directive is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Laravel Request Helper Detected as HTTP Client
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 88 asks whether request data is used dangerously in Laravel validation review. It does not import or call an HTTP client library.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text references Laravel request handling as an audit question. There is no evidence of outbound HTTP behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment Exposure Review Detected as File Access
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 49 lists reviewing .env exposure risks as an audit use case. It does not instruct the model to read local environment files or extract secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line describes a security review topic. No file read operation, secret collection, or exfiltration path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Deployment Checklist Detected as Environment File Access
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 158 asks whether a Laravel .env file is accessible through the web. This is a defensive configuration check, not local secret access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is configuration auditing. The skill contains no instruction to open, copy, or transmit an environment file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Description Text Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 3 describes OWASP and Laravel security best practices. No weak cryptographic algorithm is named or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is front matter description text. I did not find DES, MD5, SHA-1, or another weak algorithm in this location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: HTTP Code Checklist Detected as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 141 asks whether API responses use proper HTTP codes. It is unrelated to cryptographic algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text concerns HTTP status codes. There is no cryptographic operation or algorithm reference.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Security Priority Detected as Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 31 lists authorization correctness as a review priority. It does not describe scanning hosts, ports, or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The term appears in a defensive audit priority list. No reconnaissance action or target discovery behavior exists.

low_findings.title: False Positive: OWASP Awareness Detected as Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 33 lists OWASP awareness as a review priority. It is defensive methodology guidance, not network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line names a security framework. It contains no scanning command, endpoint enumeration, or external target collection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: API Abuse Review Detected as Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. SKILL.md line 75 tells the auditor to consider API abuse and rate bypass. This is threat modeling guidance for code review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is part of a threat model checklist. It does not instruct active probing or network discovery.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Combined Heuristic Did Not Hold Under Review
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The heuristic combined code execution, network, and credential-access signals, but each underlying signal is benign instructional text in SKILL.md. No evidence found of malicious intent, prompt injection, obfuscation, external command execution, network calls, or credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only scanned file is a prompt-style Markdown skill. Manual review found no executable implementation or hidden instruction that connects these static signals into a real threat.

content.value_statement: Laravel teams need focused reviews for common application security failures. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through structured Laravel security audits.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Laravel authentication, authorization, validation, and middleware patterns.
Checks common risks such as IDOR, mass assignment, SQL injection, XSS, and CSRF.
Evaluates file upload handling, API rate limiting, sensitive responses, and pagination limits.
Assesses deployment settings such as debug mode, CORS, trusted proxies, HTTPS, and .env exposure.
Structures audit responses with summary, risk level, exploit scenario, fix, and refactored example.
limitations: It is a review prompt, not an automated static analyzer.
It requires the user to provide relevant Laravel code or configuration context.
It cannot prove runtime behavior without tests, logs, or deployment access.
It focuses on Laravel 10 and 11 style applications.

use_cases.title: Controller Authorization Review
use_cases.description: Check whether controllers enforce ownership, policies, gates, and route middleware before returning protected resources.

use_cases.title: API Security Assessment
use_cases.description: Review token handling, rate limits, sensitive response fields, pagination limits, and abuse cases for Laravel APIs.

use_cases.title: Release Readiness Audit
use_cases.description: Evaluate validation, file uploads, debug settings, CORS, HTTPS, and storage exposure before production deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Laravel Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single controller or route group.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Laravel code for security issues. Focus on validation, authorization, authentication, and sensitive data exposure. Classify each finding by risk.

prompt_templates.title: API Endpoint Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of API routes and controllers.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these Laravel API endpoints for token handling, rate limiting, IDOR, pagination limits, and sensitive response data. Include exploit scenarios and fixes.

prompt_templates.title: File Upload Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of upload flows and storage configuration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this Laravel upload flow. Check MIME validation, extension rules, storage disk choice, path safety, executable upload risk, and size limits.

prompt_templates.title: Production Security Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced release review across code and configuration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a Laravel production security audit using OWASP and Laravel best practices. Cover auth, validation, database access, APIs, XSS, CSRF, uploads, and deployment settings.

output_examples.input: A controller loads an order by ID and returns it to any authenticated user.
output_examples.output: Finding: Missing ownership check. Risk: High. Impact: A user may access another user's order. Fix: Scope the query to the authenticated user or enforce a policy.

output_examples.input: An upload endpoint validates file size but stores files on a public disk without checking MIME type.
output_examples.output: Finding: Incomplete upload validation. Risk: Medium. Impact: Unsafe files may become publicly reachable. Fix: Validate MIME type, extension, size, and storage path.

output_examples.input: A production configuration has debug mode enabled and broad CORS origins.
output_examples.output: Finding: Insecure deployment configuration. Risk: High. Impact: Error details and cross-origin access may expose sensitive data. Fix: Disable debug mode and restrict allowed origins.

best_practices: Provide controllers, routes, models, policies, requests, and relevant configuration together.
State the deployment environment so severity does not rely on assumptions.
Ask for Laravel-native mitigations before adding external packages.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to audit non-Laravel applications.
Do not treat its review as a replacement for testing or professional penetration testing.
Do not provide isolated snippets when the authorization or middleware context is required.

faq.question: Does this skill run a security scanner?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI review of Laravel code and configuration that the user provides.

faq.question: Which Laravel versions does it target?
faq.answer: It is written for Laravel 10 and 11 style applications.

faq.question: Can it review API security?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers rate limiting, token handling, sensitive fields, pagination limits, and abuse cases.

faq.question: Can it find IDOR issues?
faq.answer: It can review code for missing ownership checks, weak policies, and unsafe direct object access.

faq.question: Does it need project context?
faq.answer: Yes. Better results require routes, controllers, models, policies, requests, and relevant configuration.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found no executable code, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection attempts.
